_publications._publicationTitle: C-Fiber Loss as a Possible Cause of Neuropathic Pain in Schwannomatosis.

_contextSnippet: MRN indicated abnormalities in larger peripheral nerves (lumbosacral plexus, sciatic nerve, tibial, nerve, peroneal nerve, sural nerve) in 16 of 18 patients. These abnormalities ranged from small T2-weighted lesions of individual fascicles without caliber increase to large compressive schwannoma formations.
details: MRN indicated abnormalities in larger peripheral nerves in 16 of 18 patients. Larger intraneural macrolesions or intermediate-sized nerve nodules were detected in 10 of 18 patients. Six patients had only fascicular nerve microlesions <5mm diameter. Two patients had no detectable nerve lesions.
